channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: I F U C KING SUFFER IN DENVER REEEEEEEEEE — 48 replies — 192.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I F U C KING SUFFER IN DENVER REEEEEEEEEE

Open thread • Replies: 48 • Updated: 2026-05-02 15:18 UTC

OP

I F U C KING SUFFER IN DENVER
REEEEEEEEEE

Early replies (first 3)

62173936
>>62173926
Trust the plan goy

62173941
>>62173926
gas in california is over $6 right now, you have it easy all things considered

62174058
>>62173926
Its 9.35 in Germany if you calculate it in your weird metric. But the median net salary is only 40k dollar for ful time work.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62174977
>>62174944
>texas
creo tu eres un joto.

62174998
>>62173926
I voted for this

62175538
>>62174118
Yes it's very vibrant and uhm dark
>>62174126
Well not me

62175544
this is just the beginning, and this is why the current stock valuation is tarded. just wait

62175608
>>62173926
>Dinosaur in logo
"Fossil fuels"(LOL) Yes, goy, thissss oil is preciousssss

62175657
>>62174058
It's 10.33 in Israel.

62175885
>>62174944
All this effort for the most retarded post I’ve seen this week. Bravo

62175898
Good.
Let the wealth gap grow.

62176176
>>62174951
>What part of Texas is in hell, buddy?
None of it. Texas is God's country. Also, which part of
>You did this to yourself
did you fail to understand?

62176178
>>62176176
tejas is a brown shithole.

62176179
>>62173926
gas was $3.50 and now its $4.50 and thats the reason why your life is falling apart

62176310
>>62176179
yes

62176338
I know who you are OP

62176370
>>62173926
The stock market is at ATH. You lost, tranny

62176398
I really dont know why this is so bad to you. My monthly gas bill went up by like $40. Are you guys really that strapped every month that you cant afford a $40 increase?

62176486
>>62173926
Only trannies and mexicans live in Denver.

62176488
>>62176176
Good god, Texas needs to fix their education system

62176510
Commiefornians flooded your state right before Obummer ran for office, and now you're hurting from it.
> inb4 "TRUMP CAUSED THIS"
Well no shit, point I'm making here is the blue states have even higher gas prices compared to others thanks to outrageous environmentalist laws from the commie mofos.

62176513
>>62173926
It just hit $4 in New Hampshire. Nobody even cares that I am a man trying on woman's clothing in the fitting room anymore. They all sense the same thing.

62176514
>>62173926
Cheap

62176819
>>62174083
> germany makes 0 oil
> germany imports all of its oil and energy
> germany pays out the ass
many such cases

62176841
>>62173926
Everyone at work is complaining about gas. I said "what's the problem?". They all looked at me weird.

Not my problem you losers drive Ford F-350s with $700/mo payments and work is 45 minutes from your house.

62177237
>>62176841
I drive an old camry

62177310
>>62173926
DAMMIT NOW I NEED MORE THAN TREE FIDDY

62178780
>Denver
Lafayette Costco Chads stay winning

62178995
>>62173926
Its $5.20 here faggot

62180531
You didn't buy a hybrid? Thought we all did that?

62181232
>>62173926
It's higher California you have it easy

62181234
>>62174058
well oil is evil so you guys win i guess lmao

62181239
>>62174944
>vote have an impact on whatever
D
U
M
B

F
U
C
K
I
N
G

N
I
G
G
E
R

item.title: The US national debt is now bigger than the entire US economy Today's debt milestone is not borne from war mobilization. — 30 replies — 120.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
The US national debt is now bigger than the entire US economy Today's debt milestone is not borne from war mobilization.

Open thread • Replies: 30 • Updated: 2026-05-02 15:30 UTC

OP

The US national debt is now bigger than the entire US economy
Today's debt milestone is not borne from war mobilization. It is the accumulated result of tax cuts without matching reductions in spending, an aging population drawing more heavily on Social Security and Medicare, and years of political leadership on both sides of the aisle that has consistently chosen short-term popularity over long-term solvency.
You fags are cooked (including me)

Early replies (first 3)

62179629
Doesn’t matter, because no one can collect from the US Govt

Refute this without sounding mad

62179630
>>62179622
breaking news: voters want stuff and dont want to pay for it

62179853
>>62179622
AI will fix this

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62179912
>>62179622
Interest and entitlements is almost 100% of revenue what do you cut thay woukd be meaningful exactly, I was in the camp that thought rates should never have been raised as high and definitely should have been cut it being the biggest inflation driver

62179921
>>62179622
It was designed that. It's a feature.

62179928
>>62179622
Come and take the debt, you will quickly find out why nobody has ever invaded the United States. Imagine 300 million Americans using guerilla warfare tactics, you would have to fight for every inch of this country. We will never pay that bullshit imaginary money back and there is genuinely nothing you can fucking do about it and this goes to every europoor trash on this website

62179957
>>62179629
china might beable2 doe
but then i don't really know if that's how national debt works in general, ur post seems off-topic

62179961
>>62179928
this nigga is either psyopping or psyopped
i don't understand enough about the world to pick which one
but i wish this board were better

62179968
wish i understood this shit
wish i understood the federal reserve
wish i understood glowies & moloch cults

62179970
>>62179622
I'm a goldminer chad. I'm going to make an obnoxious amount of money when this war is over.

Feels good.


Later losers.

62180005
>>62179970
>I'm going to make an obnoxious amount of money when this war is over.
which war & why would that make you make money

62180031
>>62179928
Pretty sure Pancho Villa is the only person ever successful invade the US anon.

62180037
>>62179928
Tell me you have no idea what the national debt means without telling me you have no idea what the national debt means.

62180050
You forgot
>larpers online claiming to make 500k a year working as surgeons, while most goyim living in the real world can't even get a stable non-contract office job

62180083
>>62180037
explain what it means then
>>62180050
>goyim
no such thing

62180210
>>62179928
do you understand that a default would mean a total economic collapse?

62180252
>>62179928
This. No one can fuck with Americans. Try to fuck our economy over and we will nuke your ass to next January.

62180253
>>62179961
>>62180031
>>62180037
>>62180210
Beautiful cope as if we wouldn't just invade your shit holes and take all your shit
>Muh debt!!!!

62180255
>>62179928
>300 Million
>All siding with the government that fucked them over

You sure about that?

62180256
>>62180253
Holy shit are you fucking retarded? YOU own the debt. Foreign holders a minority. Fucking braindead low iq subhuman.

62180289
>>62180255
Yes in 100% sure we would push your shit in even before you ever get the chance to step in our country

62180370
>>62180253
>>62180255
>>62180256
>>62180289
can you stop being niggers

62180372
this could be an interesting thread

62180399
>>62180255
Even if it's 100 million based on polling thats still plenty.

62180410
>>62180399
>india at 76%
that's a little staggering isn't it?

62180412
again though this is just turning into a /pol/ thread

62180442
Only serious solution:

>massively cut social security for all boomers
>massively cut medicare for all boomers
>massively cut military spending - we should only be defending our borders, not doing golem shit for Israel
>giga tax hikes for richies and actually enforce it, like hunt down and fucking kill people who hide money overseas
>eliminate all taxes for people making less than ~75k a year
>immigrating to America now costs a million dollars per person

62180452
>>62179957
No, you really cannot force another country to pay. Most you can do is steal their shit if they move it abroad. Like sone creditors did that to some Argentine boats parked in West Africa

62181251
>>62179928
>Gets hyperinflated
>Suddenly, those 300 million will fight for literally anyone that upstages the current upper class.
Not only would those 300 million not fight for the current power structure of pedophiles in this scenario, they would fight for anyone who promises slightly lower taxes now. The entire structure is a joke and everything you believe in will be upended in one election cycle.

62181263
>>62180452
This is the actual danger that retards like >>62179928
>>62180399
don't realize because they are mentally ill.

Most of the US' assets AREN'T IN THE US. Remember that whole idea of drop-shipping all of your manufacturing and industry overseas? Remember how all of these companies are hiding their real assets in tax havens in Ireland and the Netherlands? Seems it may come with some consequences for those who don't pay, doesn't it?

It's all just as well because most US debt is held by the feds, but the parts that aren't? They could just roll up to any bank and start exchanging their USD the dollar would become toilet paper overnight. Do you want $1000 sandwiches? I don't.

item.title: It's Official -- Spirit Airlines Ceases All Operations Effective Immediately At 03:00 AM EST — 25 replies — 100.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
It's Official -- Spirit Airlines Ceases All Operations Effective Immediately At 03:00 AM EST

Open thread • Replies: 25 • Updated: 2026-05-02 15:23 UTC

OP

R.I.P. to them yellow banana bois. Press F to pay respects.

https://www.spiritrestructuring.com/

Early replies (first 3)

62180860
>>62180856
rip. should be a sticky.

62180864
>>62180856
i thought orange nigger was going to bail them out with taxpayer dollars?

62180866
>>62180864
i guess you were wrong again

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62180875
>>62180864
The stipulation was that the US federal government would own a 90% stake in the company which the shareholders found to be outrageous; apparently that was the government's final offer and when it fell through that was it. They could've avoided this if they just took the Frontier merger deal the second time a year ago.

Having worked for the company a few years ago, I can tell you the day-to-day people running it were pretty cool, especially the pilots. But the entire C-suite execs were egotistical morons who spited their own employees almost any chance they got and considered us "legacy airline rejects" (actual words someone overheard a Flight Ops Manager saying).

62180892
>>62180856
This is just the start, watch all the dominoes fall after this.

62180905
>>62180892
True. More companies will start failing after this. Although Spirit was a long time coming. The people I worked with (minus management) were pretty cool though so I hope all of them will land on their feet. Seems like the Legacy carriers are going to swoop in and gobble up planes and personnel.

62180907
>>62180875
>Having worked for the company a few years ago, I can tell you the day-to-day people running it were pretty cool, especially the pilots. But the entire C-suite execs were egotistical morons who spited their own employees almost any chance they got and considered us "legacy airline rejects" (actual words someone overheard a Flight Ops Manager saying).

Thanks for your service, Spirit was fucking awesome. Always had a good experience. Got 3 total round trip nonstop flights to Vegas for about 200 dollars total, or around 35 bucks per flight, all within the past two years, absolute cheapest mode of travel ever, you guys were rockstars

62180908
>>62180856
I've flown on them twice over the past two years. The flight was dirt cheap, but after you add the simplest of luggage, the price came to about 85-90% of the other big airlines so there wasn't much savings. And there really was a difference in clients. Very much blacker than the other flights I take and nonstop loud from pre-boarding til I got to the long hallway after landing. Enough for me to pay more and fly on other airlines, it was that bad. Now they'll move on to other airlines, just have to see over time.

62180926
>>62180907
You're welcome! I was never customer-facing but my job was just as important in getting planes from point A to point B. The pilots at Spirit were especially awesome. Very chill. The gate agents may have been terse and rude but it was usually because they were horrifically understaffed, which was a management issue. You try getting hundreds of people off and on an airplane in under an hour by yourself whilst getting yelled at by entitled poors. I would be rude too lol.

The business model worked well for a time but I came in late as the wheels really started to come off. I went there with plenty of experience to go to a Legacy carrier, but I was looking forward to the JetBlue merger since I grew up in the South Florida area and wanted the benefit of Major airline pay while being near family and friends, which the merger would have facilitated. Sadly, the airline industry is extremely fickle and cyclical, so them's the brakes. I sincerely hope all my former colleagues land on their feet. The everyday working people were great, management just sucked ass.

62180929
>>62180856
F, only few them once and they fucking sucked. But at least they kept the brown hoarde at bay.

62180938
>>62180908
The trick to Spirit is (was) being able to carry a backpack on with a bunch of packing cubes then you get a round trip for only $100. It was very fun when I would fly them (even before I worked for them) because FLL always had Broward Sheriff Officers close by to the Spirit gates in order to deter chimp outs. It usually didn't work but it was fun watching blacks get suplexed or thrown off flights over very trivial things every now and then.

62180943
Never had an exceptional flight
Never had a bad flight
It was Mediocre from day 1 and almost always the cheapest option. RIP

62180945
>>62180929
Personally, I think blacks and browns will just stop flying. The Big Four have an even bigger stranglehold on the market now and already said they will raise prices due to jet fuel costs; now that there's even less route competition in the Florida market, those prices are gonna skyrocket. It's going to be too expensive for the poors to fly, which is good since in my experience ULCC flyers are 80% scum of society and 20% polite savvy consumers. I feel bad for the latter but not the former.

62180951
>>62180908
>>62180938
>The trick to Spirit is (was) being able to carry a backpack on with a bunch of packing cubes then you get a round trip for only $100

Yeah this was the move with Spirit. However, if you signed up for their credit card, you could get up to 2 free checked bags per flight. The card itself is about 70 bucks per year but the first year was free. Now that I think about it, I wonder what happens to my spirit credit card

>>62180945
I think you're right, and I could see this being a boon for bus and rail travel especially. Amtrak will probably get a lot busier for routes that were once serviced by Spirit. Of course, it also takes longer, and in the age of minimal days off with PTO, it's not often an option for people. That being said, rail is the most resilient form of transport to oil price change since it is so efficient

62180955
>>62180945
I have always used Sun country for Florida flights and always found them cheaper than spirit, and I fly there twice a year, I fly with only a backpack

62180967
>>62180951
>Now that I think about it, I wonder what happens to my spirit credit card
If you check the link in OP there's another link that describes what happens to points and such. You might be fucked though.

>I think you're right, and I could see this being a boon for bus and rail travel especially.
Good point. I'm pretty sure the Brightline saw an uptick in ticket sales when Silver Airlines went under, since they mostly serviced the South Florida region. Amtrak will probably do well.

62180971
>>62180955
Sun Country, Allegiant, Breeze, Avelo and Frontier will likely get most of Spirit's previous clientele. The Legacy carriers are just going to keep getting more expensive.

62180976
>>62180967
>If you check the link in OP there's another link that describes what happens to points and such. You might be fucked though.

I only had 7,000 points left after my most recent round flight with Spirit two weeks ago. Unless I booked something over 6 months in advance, and even then, 7,000 points was maybe good for half the cost of a single flight. I don't care. I got 6 total flights from Spirit for a grand total of 200 bucks. If I don't get any back from those points that's fine, Spirit served me well, I feel like I got a great value already

62180978
>>62180945
I hope this is true but I doubt it. They'll just go into debt to fly like they go into debt to buy SUVs and shit.
Fuck, this sucks. I live in the south so the primary airlines were Spirit and Delta. Not only will Delta surely begin raising their prices but now I'll have to deal with even more niggers

62181006
>>62180945
Lol, lmao. Nobody curtails spending when prices of things go up, especially not groids.

62181048
>>62180856
They can sell all the jet engines for AI data centers.

62181053
Pour one out for the homie.

62181069
>>62180971
Maybe that's for the best. Spirit was a great way of keeping anti social behavior off the primary airlines.

62181075
>>62181069
She's sucking her gut in so hard for the picture that it could depressurize the whole joint.

62181250
recession indicator

item.title: What can I do — 21 replies — 84.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
What can I do

Open thread • Replies: 21 • Updated: 2026-05-02 15:31 UTC

OP

I just turned 18 last week I have no job no career no college no car just graduated high school only have 3k saved. What should I do to continue living and make my way as an adult? I feel useless and incapable. I can walk 20 minutes to some places for work but it’s all fast food and they won’t even reply to my applications and I have absolutely nothing to put on resumes but I’m thinking about lying for minor achievements until I can get my first job. I’m NGMI right? What have you done to get up and become an adult in the world? Must I go to college? I live at home and have no government assistance. Also despite being a female anon I’m too ugly for sex work so no suggestions towards that thanks help me out finance anons, finally I don’t have a license but I can work towards that easily. In America.

Early replies (first 3)

62180503
>>62180499
>female
sell feet pics

62180511
>>62180499
go to hvac school

62180517
Pray.
>>>/bant/24269377

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62180519
>>62180499
22april25: 30$
toDAY: 1187$
3000>118,700

62180524
>>62180499
BUY nvax.
150 is sure.
hope 300

62180529
>>62180499
You must have a shitty home life if your parents aren't encouraging you to go to college. What you really need is a mentor (not me) to guide you towards making the best decisions at your baby age of 18.

62180561
>>62180499
tits

62180584
>>62180529
*Parent
My mom does want me to work or continue education but mainly as a precedent and because I need to be more than a legal child with money. I’m bad with finance and have a minuscule amount saved up. I wish somebody noticed I was so depressed early on like with doctor visits where I couldn’t imagine a future for myself when asked. Now I’m stuck and don’t have any proper guidance. Should I continue applying to these places I can walk to? I just don’t know. I graduated with a 2.4 (2.6 weighted) GPA and am completely useless. All I can foresee school wise is community college but I don’t know what I’ll pursue. thanks
>>62180561
I’m very ashamed and insecure and hate every single part of my body. I don’t want sex work suggestions for my life and future money. I even think my feet are ugly and fat

62180642
>>62180499
go to nursing school or the military, college is a meme in 2026 for people like you
pick up any job you can in the meantime even if its putting fries in the bag
build up cash savings
once you buy a car, buy something cheap and reliable and dont fall for the jewish car payment crap
open a brokerage account and start putting money there every month

dont be a retard along the way and youll be fine

62180644
You're literally 18. Take it from someone that spent 18 to 30 being a loser, if I could go back in time and lock in I would.

62180647
>>62180644
You just have to find it in yourself to do things that you don't want to do. Everyone would rather be rotting about then putting in the work. Its kind of trite now because of all the retarded e-celeb hustle bs but all you need is my first sentence.

62180648
>>62180499
If you're a woman and you're ugly it's impossible for you to make it. Find some guy who isn't a complete loser and just have him do everything on the off chance that he makes it and then you can divorce him, take half and use it as investment capital to live comfortably into old age.

62180650
>>62180503
>>62180584
post your feet here unironically
we can judge better than you
and i once did it as well (male btw) for fun on /vg/ and it gave me few chuckles

62180651
>>62180499
>Also despite being a female anon I’m too ugly for sex work so no suggestions
just out of curiosity - would you really consider sex work if you would be good looking for it?

62180653
>>62180650
I posted my leg hair on /v/, the reviews came in as good.

62180688
>>62180584
Sounds like you are beating yourself up way too much. Everyone is bad with finance at 18. Almost noone has 3k saved up at this age. So you are already doing better than average.

Don't ever get involved in sex work and other degrading types of ''work''.

Think about what you want to do. Work with people, or preferably not with people? Outside or inside? With computers or something else? Based on that, choose a career path, you will probably need to study in some form for that. Meanwhile keep applying to all jobs you can find. Working will be much better for your mental health than rotting at home. Many of us worked bad retail/food industry jobs at your age, then eventually moved to much better jobs.

62180701
You would fell for the wage meme and you would be full of toxic like all the femcel today.

62180719
>>62180584
someone suggested nursing, its hard but the least likely to be replaced by ai, right now you are just in a paralysis analysis mode and you need to make a decision, any decision, otherwise you might regret, you can't not make mistakes at your age, in fact this is the time to make them and learn from them

62180876
go get a degree in physics or math. yeah you have to overcome your brainletism but nerds will simp for you and you get preferential treatment for jobs as a foid

62181256
>>62180499
I turned 20 in January. I'm on my fourth job, and my parents got me two of them. Try asking your parents, a relative, or a friend for a job.

62181266
>>62180499
Women are playing life on tutorial mode.
You can always just hook up with some guy with money.
18?
Marry a 30 year old millionaire.
Easy option.
Otherwise, corporations want to hire women.
Pretend to be a whatever and they'll hire you over a vastly more qualified man.

My wife is 10 years my junior. Her sister married a guy 15 years older.

item.title: admit it linkies. admit that you're not special. all you did was buy some stupid token and got lucky. but then, in a cru… — 20 replies — 80.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
admit it linkies. admit that you're not special. all you did was buy some stupid token and got lucky. but then, in a cru…

Open thread • Replies: 20 • Updated: 2026-05-02 15:17 UTC

OP

admit it linkies. admit that you're not special. all you did was buy some stupid token and got lucky. but then, in a cruel twist of fate, it turns out the coin you devoted your life to was actually 90% hype. and now you are poor. reduced to normal faggotry and mediocrity, where you belong. admit these things and then there will be a path to redemption. but if you keep living in denial, you will wallow in your pathetic misery forever. the choice is yours. i am trying to help you. ALL of us are trying to help you.

Early replies (first 3)

62180296
>>62180275
No

62180322
>>62180275
"all of us" are using Chainlink - when Sergey compares the token to buying in to Linux, he was right.

62180323
what was with the fuddies getting so mind broken they started spamming gay porno couple nights back lmao, can't be real

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62180368
>>62180322
Nobody made money off of Linux.

62180382
>>62180275
My cousin unironically works for chainl*nk and it's fucking OVER

62180384
they're trying to force the linkbtc downtrend but they will have to crash bitcoin if they want any blood from this stone, no one is selling lmao

62180392
>>62180275
LINK is just like all the other shitty altcoin scams that fade into obscurity. The morons thought they were special

62180413
>>62180368
because linux didn't create a token

62180639
Why is this board so obsessed with chainlink?

62180669
HOLY SHIT

By sheer chance I happened to be watching Sunny today, "The gang finds a dumpster baby", and recognised one of the frames Asslarper used (pic related).

In this episode, Dennis (AB's avatar) convinces an activist to CHAINLINK himself to a tree, then LITERALLY cuckolds him.

>A SINGULAR TREE
>THE cuckOLDS OF CRYPTO!!!

Now do you believe me??

Inverse Assblaster; what did he tell you was useless?

62180680
>entire fud campaign based on some vc seething about ((("being wrong")))
kek

62180684
>>62180275
Hello mentally ill faggot how are you doing today?

62180685
>>62180639
99% of this boards wealth is in LINK

62180698
Its gonna be fine
People were dunking on the chainlink build rewards, rightly so because of value, but they also laughed because we had to do a quiz.

Turns out that chainlink knew about legislative action months ago before it actually got settled. We're in the whitehouse, we're gonna get our commodity status, basically every prediction market that matters adopted link. If one could argue there is one infra play in crypto, its chainlink. The only thing you're betting on is how big the institutional crypto market is gonna get, which at that point is a sure bet of substantial increase no matter what copers will say

62180700
>>62180680
>>62180684
>>62180323
hey cuckolds, check the price :)

62180702
>>62180698
Amundis spiko fund is secured by chainlink and has arguably been one of the winners in that category, its gonna be fine

Lots of replies will be just straight up coping by fudders who hold link themselves

62180728
>>62180413
If they did, it would be as useless as BAT

62180736
>>62180680
>they were wrong
>$9
insane cuckold cope

62180801
>>62180275
israel won

62181236
>>62180698
>they also laughed because we had to do a quiz.
rightly so, stinkbag

item.title: Charizard > HAWK — 30 replies — 67.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Charizard > HAWK

Open thread • Replies: 30 • Updated: 2026-05-02 15:05 UTC

OP

You could have purchased Pokémon cards instead of getting rug pulled on a shitcoin.

Early replies (first 3)

62177375
>>62177352
grown men trying to offload their plastic bing bing wahoos onto unsuspecting children who think it's a "hobby" is hilarious

62177465
>>62177352
A girl I work with has a side business selling pokemon cards. Annually, she makes more money from that than her salary.
We work in fucking finance.
I pray for the bubble to pop just for the world to make sense again.

62177475
>>62177352
>The world has devolved to the point where grown men are trading toys for money
Uncle Ted was right this whole time.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62177523
>>62177465
I could never do that shit, humiliation ritual all the way battling other man children in isles for pokemon boxes, I would probably be sent to prison after the first time I try to do it

62177554
>>62177352
>7 years
a single crypto project got me 30K after holding for just over 1 year

62177645
What if you bought nvidia though

62177651
>>62177352
i should have listened to that zard autist a year ago

62177966
>first pack i ever opened was a holo charizard 1st edition in 1995 or w/e
>probably rotting in a landfill or my parents basement whom i've cut all contact with
shucks

62178287
*inhales*
Pokemon cards are an alternate form of currency which has all the features of currency while resting outside the functional reach of the government. Think of it as art collections for poor people. An inflated micro economy of money laundering through paintings etc. Well poor people have done the same thing with trading cards. Combine this with the fact that these little scraps of paper represent market share in the most successful entertainment franchise in history, have scarcity, suffer natural attrition, and are strongly associated with psychological tendencies like herd mentality and hoarding. Rich people are so greedy that they ALWAYS become envious of any niche club that gains value without them, so here come the fucking city slickers to inject a fuckton of liquidity into the trading card market since 2020.
This was part of clown world programming and you should have seen it coming from a mile away.

62178305
>>62177375
Buying stuff on eBay and opening pokemon-themed scratch off tickets is a perfectly valid hobby chud

62178416
>>62177352
Is this all what Pokémon is now? Greed? A pyramid scheme ending in a rugpull?

Very sad!

62178433
>>62177352
Is this for real? That tag team didn't see much play.

62178468
>>62178416
If you are speculating that this ends in a rugpull, there's no evidence to support that theory. Crypto has rotted your brain so that you think every non-traditional investment is a scheme, but you overlook the fact that people genuinely want to own these luxury assets which appear to hold value across generations.

62178472
>>62178433
It's been a trending valentines/anniversary day gift among the ultra wealthy for a few years. Nothing to do with playability.

62178476
>>62177352
good luck finding retards to sell them to, pokebaggie

62178520
>>62178468
Nta but these are entirely worthless novelty items. Are you stupid enough to think that someone bought this piece of crap for 20000K cause they thought it was worth that much? Obviously they will try to sell it later for even more absurd prices.

And if the buyer was an insane collector, that doesnt mean this card can reliably sold on the market either.

62178643
>>62178520
You sound like you're in disbelief of the events happening around you.

>someone bought this piece of crap for 20000K cause they thought it was worth that much

Have you ever seen gemstone auctions? People's value perception is skewed and you're the idiot for not turning this fact to your advantage

62178716
>>62177966
Make amends with your parents you selfish retard

62178782
>>62178520
i mean art is worthless but has had value for thousands of years anon

think of it as investing in a watch or art

62178810
>>62178716
them first
but also no

62178813
>>62177352
Always buy things with a proven track record. Recently I've been branching into 19th century rare books and japanese import pornographic switch games to diversify my collectables. Did over $20,000 dollars in sales on ebay last year using some boxes from this pic.

62178815
>>62178716
Why is the internet constantly full of pathetic faggots telling people to spend time with psycho boomer parents? Take your weird reddit chungus mentality and kill yourself in the most brutal way possible. If someone cuts ties with a boomer it's definitely because the boomer is tremendously fucked in the head, faggot.

62178823
>>62178815
because destroying the concept of a tightly bonded family is (((their))) goal.

62178989
>>62178716
>Make amends with the people who treated you like shit

You MUST be a boomer.

62179014
>>62177966
The insanely high valuations are only for cards in mint condition, so unless you put it in a sleeve right away it won't be worth that much anyway. Perfect grade holo 1st edition holo Charizard go for like 6-7 figures

62179017
leave it to materialism threads to reveal the types of angsty losers who forsake their family and would rather give money to a jewish lawfirm

62180661
>>62178476
>boring history based collectibles for boomers with dead players that kids never saw and can't relate with + limited to US market
vs
>cool fantasy collectibles for kids with active player base growing every game release + worldwide market
I don't think you get the difference, I'm not a Pokémon collector but it's obvious that it won't die until the video game license and cards competition scene doesn't collapse, kids nowadays still care about Pokémon, I'm European (whiter than you) and I don't know any players on your picrel

62180921
>>62178810
>>62178815
>>62178989
It’s for your own good, not theirs. This retard says he has a 1st Ed Charizard but he refuses to go back and take it. Trapped in a downward cycle of resentment. Those who don’t forgive others only punish themselves. Perfect example of this here. And forgiveness doesn’t mean you have to get along with that person. Just humble yourself and get over it. If you can’t do that, your pride and ego is too big.

62181195
>>62178468
This is like that scam in RuneScape where someone is "buying" something useless for 100k and someone else sees it and rushes to buy the item for a bit cheaper to sell it to them

I bet these "expensive" trades are just like that .. groups "selling" to each other to hype the price up and then some poor sucker is left holding the bag, but you made them both 10k richer kek

62181204
>>62177352
Yeah ok, in theory... because who the fuck actually buys Pokemon cards in 2026?

62179895

TTP

62179899
>>62179889

62180002
>>62179899
I got railed pretty hard in late 2023 and haven't touched crypto since. I have never been happier.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62180027

62180063
it's going to zero. they will blame congress to deflect from themselves

62180347

62180350

62180351

62180430

62180471

62180508
kek i made like 50k off this the day it went to 100,
sucks to be you baggies

62180545

62180582
>>62180545
you're fucking retarded, it tapped 100 dollars. i sold at 65.

item.title: Is it worse to be a wagie or a NEET? — 181 replies — 202.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Is it worse to be a wagie or a NEET?

Open thread • Replies: 181 • Updated: 2026-05-02 14:38 UTC

OP

Is it worse to be a wagie or a NEET?

Early replies (first 3)

62158601
>>62158557
Neet
Wagie can get some money to go out and have fun, and socialize.
might not be your cup of tea, until you making out with a 8/10 girl who you just said hi to, 2 minutes ago

62158623
>>62158601
>might not be your cup of tea, until you making out with a 8/10 girl who you just said hi to, 2 minutes ago

while waging at walmart is impossible

62158626
>>62158557
I prefer to be a neet, but ofc neither situation is perfect. Now if i was getting pussy then we'd be talking

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62169348
>>62167534
>I only have one perspective so my perspective is right
Great now I know not to take your opinion seriously.

62169412
>>62158557
It's worse to be a wagie, obviously. Why would anyone ever think being NEET is worse? The whole point of working is to save up enough money to be NEET someday.

62169467
>>62169412
Yes and waging gets you more money and expedites that process so you can eventually neet. If you go neet mode with no money you're just suffering and can't truly enjoy life, and the "enjoyment" you have is just consuming media which makes you as much of a goy as a wagie.

62169540
>>62159267
Kierkegaard was based Dane, like Schacht.

62170345
>>62158557
depends on your situation. neet is better when you can actually afford it. if you need money then having a job is better no matter how soul crushing it is most of the time.

62170481
>>62159382
At least you know what checked actually means unlike that other dude.
But honestly man, why would my view be goyish? I am just saying biology will motivate you in life aka a hot woman
>>62169232
Fuck yeah dude. A hot woman keep saying I love you while pulling your hair, moaning and can’t stop herself from kissing you? Yeah I don’t care what she did with who before at that moment
>>62169261
Bro I had a one full terabyte of Skyrim and it’s mod enable on my shit laptop and was playing it without issue, so I have no trouble going around my laptop limitation. I could entertain myself with two absolutly simple games like xcom piretez or dominion 6 (both that I loved) then when I got bored I would used chatgpt to make assets and modded the game to my taste myself and started over, so no, I know how to not get bored either.
Problem is that a shit laptop is just not able to handle all the calculation of ck3 or stellaris (thanks to paradox shit programming) and total war had to be played on low settings.
I don’t like that shit. I don’t wanna be limited like this. Why would I limit myself to some fake and not my favorite, while I can go work, make some money, and get a pc that can handle ALL MY GAMES?
>>62169467
Yep. A smart wagie is a free man.
my problem with being a neet was always lack of safety net, but a wagie has that and he can get out of rat race faster.
>>62169412
Sure, we all love to be neet. But being a retired YOUNG neet, with money and your own car and place, is hard fucking work. (Or luck)
You can either sit on your ass and wish to be like that, have a good luck in lotto or stocks or crypto or work your ass and wage. In the end, the wagie gonna win, because he got more money to dump into stocks and crypto while a neet doesnt.
And god, I fucking WISHED I had my own place right now dude.

62170488
>>62170481
It’s still me, I don’t know why my id keep changing, probably cause I am a phonefag posting at work

62170497
Either has their pros and cons. Waging can get enraging and demoralizing but is a wonderful source of social, schedule, productivity, and income. Neeting has freedom and self-expression but can get so isolating and repetitive that it can make you break down mentally entirely over time.

Neeting and waging are two sides of the same coin. Balance in all things and you will find peace.

62171998
>>62158557
NEETs have dignity.

62175198
>>62158601
Skill issue. I'm working on become turboneet through three sources and will clear 93-95k when they are approved.

62175347
>>62170481
>>62170345
Almost everyone that owns a house in a top tier city came from well off families. I don’t think the majority truly realize how important family help is and how big the wealth gap is.
Good houses in good areas are truly unaffordable for normal or people in the bottom 95%.

62175361
>>62170497
> Either has their pros and cons. Waging can get enraging and demoralizing but is a wonderful source of social, schedule, productivity, and income. Neeting has freedom and self-expression but can get so isolating and repetitive that it can make you break down mentally entirely over time.
There is nothing productive about waking up at 6am everyday. Neet is a choice but waging isn’t. I think this is what no one wants to say. No one working would choose to work if they didn’t have to work.

62175389
>>62158557
Diogenes, Buddha and Jesus was Neets
nobody remembers the trillions of wagies
being a wagie is worser than being a neet

62175537
>>62167410
the real best of both worlds is WFH wagie
>forced into a regular wake up schedule that isn’t obscenely early or late
>no commute
>less oversight allows you to better enjoy the downtime inherent to any job, without giving you so much free time as to burn out media quickly
>access to your own kitchen during breaks and all the wagie bucks to buy good ingredients
>once again no commute, allowing you to instantly switch to leisure the moment the workday is done

62176675
>>62168012
https://soul-zone.org/

62177246
>>62158601
40 years ago maybe

62177573
>>62168700
>when a girl you just saw is now CRAZY into you, to the point of saying she loved you, keep trying to makeout with you, is just all over you
pointless to think about because it has never happened and will never happen

62177728
neets arent masculine and no one respects them

62179485
At least neets get to sleep.

62179529
neets because there's nothing like realizing you wasted your whole damn life and also you're a lazy piece of shit that should've just killed yourself

62179583
>>62179529
i genuinely don't know whether you're talking about wagies or neets

62179651
>>62168700
> social media lied to me
> i didn’t participate in it
> mfw
I’m not saying you’re choosing wrong, not everyone is suited to NEET life and the world needs productive people.
But maybe just have some faith in yourself that you were making your own bad choices and then turned it around when you found motivation.

62179923
After having neet'maxxd and wage cage'd and finally landed at a job I actually don't mind that pays very well the last is infinitely preferable to both of the former, but I'd put NEET above any true wagie job. I see some people act like a mundane office job is wagie work and I just assume they've never had to work in a truly dead end hellscape position. There are some truly soul-crushing jobs out there.

62179934
>>62165202
Being able to show up at the same time every day is a skill. Dealing with people is a skill.

62179952
>>62166566
It's ok anon. You picked yourself up. I fucked off in my 20s. I wasn't a neet but I have nothing to show for it. Didn't save any money. Thankfully I did enough to get into a good law school and I just graduated at 33. But it does suck being years behind people who are younger than me and making partner. However you have years and years of career ahead of you. Don't look back.

62179958
>>62166748
People who had parents who can leave an inheritance worth anything are generally successful. You're a crab.

62180244
>>62179934
Seriously. Going to work everyday day is a skill. So tempting to just take a day off.

62180261
>>62177246
>>62177573
Ok guys
I gonna go tonight again.
and next day.
If I didn’t end up making out with a hot a girl like last time, I will get back here and tell you it might have been a fluke
>>62179651
I said that because tons of guys are also getting blasted by social media and just gave up

62180686
>>62180261
Alright night one reporting in
Fail
On to night 2 tomorrow

62181149
>>62158557
Neet
>t. Neet
If you're a wagie at least you can have a social life and if it's something like working at a bar you can easily meet girls too

item.title: Woman JP Morgan executive Rapes and turns Male Employee into sex slave — 155 replies — 157.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Woman JP Morgan executive Rapes and turns Male Employee into sex slave

Open thread • Replies: 155 • Updated: 2026-05-02 14:33 UTC

OP

https://x.com/dailymail/status/2049618941043949691?s=46

A white woman JP Morgan Executive (mega international bank) drugged and raped a married man before taking pictures of him to use as blackmail.

She would force him to take drugs like viagra so she could fuck him longer. She started buying him gifts which as sex toys including anal ones which she forced him to use and got him a position where she had direct control over him and he acted as her assistant. She also pegged him.

He was crying over the situation and talking to authorities who didn’t believe him.

She was caught when police investigated her for ties to Trump and Epstein with her blackmailing the employee being unclassified.

Early replies (first 3)

62172413
>>62172412

62172414
>>62172413
kek

62172420
>>62172413
post her tits are they big and nice?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62176970
>>62176949
She did know him and he tried to do an internal complaint but then refused to actually answer any questions when the company investigated LMAO

62176973
>>62175043
I use it all the time and now I’m sad that I can’t go to warosu and search “fish head” and see exclusively my posts now

62176986
>>62176674
Smoke show

62176988
>>62176674
Look at those assets
No wonder she works at a bank

62176994
>>62175043
she's albanian not white

62176996
>>62175827
When the bank says there's nothing there that's pretty bad.
Also he's Indian so it's clearly fanfiction.

62177044
>>62174634
>>62174688
Basically this, it was a jeet writing his own fanfiction on his laptop but he forgot to delete them before they were checked out by LEO

62177061
>>62172448
dudes, i've never felt so abandoned by god as i do in this moment

62177089
>>62172412
what the fuck is wrong with this faggot? if this shit happened me i'd quit my job to solely be her live in fuckmeat. i'd do all the weird nasty shit she'd be into as well like huffing her farts in a gas mask.

>>During the second encounter, Hajdini allegedly ordered him to suck her toes, pushed him to the ground and sat on his face, berating him when he was unable to sustain arousal.

I don't even like toes either dude but you'd bet your fucking ass i'd be cleaning every grain of dirt off of those fucking things and smelling the fuck out of them. fucking loser faggot

62177151
lmao this is exactly how Indians think white women are. It's so obvious that it's jeet delusions

62177156
>>62177089
This is all pajeet bullshit lies. Why would you trust anyone who shits in the street.

62177231
>>62172412
Aaaaaaand it turns out the jeet literally made it all up.

62179620
>>62177089
>I don't even like X but I would do X
get your T tested, see if you're able to do a flip off a bridge.

62179655
>>62174634
>show bobs and vagene
let a man dream

62180085
>>62172412
It's fake.
How do I know?
1. No one in the US calls Indians Asians.
Let alone calling an Indian guy "little brown Asian boy"
2. He claims to have a Asian wife, but doesn't in reality.
It's buck breaking 2.0 SAARS edition

That women will forever be blasted by that fake civil suit.

62180177
fake and gay

62180263
>>62173170
You were right.

62180643
>>62174634
This sounds very fake.
I suggest that alternatively
>Jeet with wife sexually harassed his woman boss
>Is denied and loses 3000 izzat
>Makes up this story to recoup his losses

62180818
>>62176986
It's AI you mong

62180821
>>62173364
>pending

FFS leave her alone.


>>62176674

Is that real?

62180823
>>62172454
Literally younger jack ma with a wig

62180838
>>62172412
Whats the deal with her, is she married/kids?
Is she really Albanian?

62180849
>>62174807
>western women in japan or thailand getting no dates

lol. they would 100% get tons of dates from asian men who want a blonde gf. but they are probably just picky bitches who want for a 10/10 western expat chad

62180851
>>62172412
>He was crying over the situation and talking to authorities who didn’t believe him.
Yeah, nobody else believes him either.

>saar my benis is so yuge that white bitch demand it from me saar

>ties to Trump and Epstein
Then why was she going after a guy of legal age?

62180859
>>62180849
>they are probably just picky bitches
Yep. I lived in Taiwan for most of the last 20 years, and the two things every white male always heard from Western female expats was (1) they can't get dates because all the white guys have yellow fever, and (2) "eww the local guys have tiny dicks and don't make enough money to date me!"

In all fairness the Taiwanese guys do have tiny dicks tho.

62180991
>>62180975
not funny

62180994
>>62172412
THIS MAN IS NOT INDIAN HE IS PAKISTAN PLEASE STOP WITH THE SPREADING OF MISINFORMATION IT IS VERY DAMAGING

62181004
>>62180991
Get sprayed, bug

62181132
>>62175811

item.title: >survive the deadliest earthquake in recent history >parents sent to labor camps, grew up in orphanages >lead the Tianan… — 18 replies — 45.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
>survive the deadliest earthquake in recent history >parents sent to labor camps, grew up in orphanages >lead the Tianan…

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-05-02 15:08 UTC

OP

>survive the deadliest earthquake in recent history
>parents sent to labor camps, grew up in orphanages
>lead the Tiananmen protests
>flees to US, learns English
>writes a book that forms the basis of a movie
>attends Columbia University, earns three degrees simultaneously
>hears Warren Buffett give a lecture on investing
>immediately studies value investing
>uses the float of his student loans to invest in companies
>makes a million dollars while still at college, solely through investments
>becomes one of the greatest value investors in history

Li Lu is GOATed.

Early replies (first 3)

62176696
>>62176684
Yeah boomers had if easy, what's your point?

62176701
Why he look kinda minnie pooh, you sure he is not shady and backed by connection?

62177096
>>62176684
>earns three degrees simultaneously
American education is a joke lmao, I've met a PhD that had no master degree, what a fool kek

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62177145
>>62177096
This is a joke, right?
It hasn't been a linear progression of those degrees since the 60ss
Now the biggest difference is:
>you pay the university for a master's
>they pay you for a PhD
>t. specifically dodged the shit job market in 2008 to get a PhD

62177150
>>62176684
Based.

62177171
>>62176696
Saying this is exactly the same thing as a boomer saying, "why don't you just play video games on twitch and become a millionaire! It's so easy!"

62179868
Bump.

62180026
>>62179868
for what purpose?
>>62176684
interesting
>>uses the float of his student loans to invest in companies
what does this mean, btw?

62180072
>>62180026
>what does this mean, btw?

He was given money periodically to pay for his tuition and board. Instead of doing this, he invested it. By the time he had to pay for tuition and board, he had made more than enough from his investments to cover it. Risky, unless you have very high confidence in your bets.

62180075
>>62176684
He is the La Li Lu Le Lo

62180137
>>62180072
oh i see
i didn't think it would be physically possible to spend student loan money that way xd
ty anon

62180587
>>62176696
>See a one-in-a-million example of hardworking boomers.
>Immediately trashtalking
Yeah, I would like to believe the IQ test in your country was too expensive for your parents to take you here and find out you are a retarded faggot

62180665
>>62176684
Boomers had it easy

62180956
>>62180665
Why does everyone say this? The opportunities young people have now dwarf those of boomers. They're just in weird self-defeating mind prisons as a result of so many online psyops.

62181051
>>62180137
It isn't anymore
One, because they don't want the opportunity for you to get ahead.
Two, because enough people (still a minority) invested their loan funds and lost their shirt doing so, banks got the big mad. Since banks control everything, that had to change

62181097
>>62180075
what a thrill

62181119
>>62180956
for boomers the stock market was like crypto, companies would take off all the time, and even if you're particular company didn't do well it could get bought out by a company that was doing well and you could still make money. investing only a little bit in home depot or any of the companies they bought out back when they started would've made you a millionaire by the 2000s, back when a million dollars meant something. you think programming makes you rich now? back in the 90's 3 guys could build one website and get bought out and never work ever again, which happened to my moms friends husband. boomers could get a job with zero qualifications to not only make rent but have enough left over to invest in the market every paycheck. having a college degree of any kind in the 70's basically put you in the top 10% of earners.

62181213
>>62180956
The opportunities people have today are 0

Boomers could fall over backwards and still win

item.title: siriusly bros — 15 replies — 19.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
siriusly bros

Open thread • Replies: 15 • Updated: 2026-05-02 14:47 UTC

OP

we had the PC, Internet, Smartphone, and then Bitcoin and AI.. each wave stripped out gatekeepers and reshaped entire industries.
If Bitcoin is disrupting money itself the way the Internet disrupted information, what’s the next layer to fall?

What’s the next asymmetric play that’s still early, still ignored, and actually changes the base layer of how things work?

Early replies (first 3)

62176136
>>62176117
Robots reaching the level of adroitness of a Walmart wagie combined with AI about as good as it is today. It's fucking Joever then.

>Walmart wagie
>McSlop bowl assembly, most food prep
>Door dash
>Amazon slop shoveler
>Uber driver
>Warehouse employees
>Most customer service people

At that point tens of millions of people run out of anything valuable to do. And that's when government dollar collapse and people get hungry and angry. Hopefully you have a compound at that point and can afford armed robosentries

62176154
how did having a phone in your hand ot make tiktoks and youtube videos 'disrupted' dosiety

62176208
>>62176117
>Bitcoin is disrupting money itself
Schizo.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62176495
>>62176117
data is still in its infancy
>still not encrypted by default
>still taking a long time to encode/decode
>still taking a lot of space
>still slow to validate

62176545
>>62176136
> people get hungry and angry
Fuck them.
There is also definitely some fuckery happening in the market these past weeks and it will definitely devalue the dollar and cause inflation

62176681
Genetic engineering. CRISPR baby already won the Olympics

62176826
>>62176117
>If Bitcoin is disrupting money itself the way the Internet disrupted information

62176866
i am disappoint

62177264
>>62176117
Who is that snu snu beast? Is that AI?

62179052
>>62177264
She's about 5'3 those are normal sized Chinese.

62180894
>>62179052
okay but who is it?

62180913
>>62180894
contribute 1st.
supreme dream asian vagene 2nd.

62180917
>>62180913
holy fuck she is real? tineye didn't find anything so I chalked it up to gen AI

62181140
>>62180917
> What’s the next asymmetric play that’s still early, still ignored, and actually changes the base layer of how things work?
mebbe i can arrange for a sharpie in the poop for you

62181170
>>62181140
okay this image convinced me it's AI

what a pity

item.title: Give this man a medal. — 13 replies — 11.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Give this man a medal.

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-05-02 14:23 UTC

OP

Give this man a medal.

https://youtu.be/awKjusa-JFQ?si=5CsS9ctd3wDASWzE&t=204

Early replies (first 3)

62180541
Lonely loser rant videos like this keep showing up on my youtube feed. I don't understand why since I'm financially successful and have a girlfriend. These videos might have made sense for me like 10 years ago.

62180589
>>62180541
I guess financial success can destroy your sense of humour, since that guy is a comedian, obviously you thought it was real.

62180611
>>62180478
>normgroid NPCs think this is clever, sophisticated and funny

We need to kill like 6 billion "people". These beings are not sentient and obviously not ensouled, and yes that includes OP.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62180621
>>62180478
>>62180611
>all those fucking cringe comments on Youtube from non-sentient bots

It's like turning on the kitchen light at night and seeing a bunch of cockroaches scuttle around. OP deserves to be shot in the head for even linking this disgusting freak's awful video.

62180631
>>62180478
that video is making fun of retarded niggers who dig their own graves btw
if you relate to it on any real level please check yourself before the jews & demons you cannot see/understand wreck you

62180764
>>62180478
>I couldnt find an answer do i didnt do any of it
>Cant relate the fact that his life is shit cause he didnt do any of the stuff that "had no point"
Lol goycattle really are funny.

62180831
>>62180541
I have the same issue.
Its happening because the algorithm has been bought by faggots who want us to be blackpilled.
They make up all the excuses like "we dont know how the algorithm works" talking about social media but they very clearly do since it has been changed to accomodate different types of content not usually seen.

62180853
>>62180478
Well, the video is better than the alternative. Postal workers are interesting.

62180885
>>62180478
profoundly based and deeply red pilled, if you disagree with him then you don't fucking get it. We should be able to live freely, not as slaves

62180915
YouTube is being taken over by "life is shit" speeches, I watched a few out of curiosity and I don't disagree entirely but it's all I see now I've injected them in my algorithm.
We are in a shit time, for me especially but many of them don't help themselves in any way, just pure whine.

62180941
>>62180885
You can go innawoods today nothing is stopping you, you low IQ savage hippie.

62181029
>>62180915
Go into your history and delete it. Algorithm curation is crucial. Watch 2 Andrei Jikh and Jaime Ibanez videos to /biz/set your algo

62181099
>>62180478

item.title: I have a new coworker at my blue collar job. — 11 replies — 7.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
I have a new coworker at my blue collar job.

Open thread • Replies: 11 • Updated: 2026-05-02 14:06 UTC

OP

I have a new coworker at my blue collar job. We work on computerized high voltage systems. He apparently has an EE degree but our job is very much more maintenance focused and he is, quite frankly, lost. Another sticking point is that the official procedures we are supposed to follow are mostly bullshit desu.
He also is legacy US Chinese and obviously autistic.
Saying something like "Well the procedure says this but what *I* do to achieve the same goals is ..." just flies over his head unless you really hammer it home.
The biggest issue is that he wants to try to solve every little issue that comes up, even when we tell him that that's either something that we, the senior techs, know will never change; or that the thing he has an issue with is literally part of the job he signed up for.
Basically how do I deal with an autist who is over-educated and inexperienced?
Pic unrelated

Early replies (first 3)

62177365
>>62177347
>something that we, the senior techs, know will never change
Tbqh from the outside sounds like you guys have the issue here. If there's something that's inherently confusing and held together by tribal knowledge you should fix this.

>or that the thing he has an issue with is literally part of the job he signed up for
That's on him. Just be clear

Anyway get drunk the fucker and break down barriers is my suggestion.

62177405
>>62177365
Tribal knowledge is exactly one of the issues. How do you pass that to someone who seemingly has to be first taught that I, as a senior employee, can only hint at what what he should actually be focusing on.

62177415
>>62177347
He will either learn to listen or he won't. It is a common problem among new grad engineers that they discount tribal knowledge in favor of textbook knowledge. They will usually figure out how wrong they are, given the chance. If he's autistic he might not. I'd give it 3 months for him to either figure it out or be fired.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62177459
what the fuck is legacy US chinese...is that like a descendant of a railroad worker

62177474
>>62177347
>>62177365
>>62177415
>>62177365
>>62177459
Unskilled laborers on here love to make their job sound more complex than it really is. You can tell they're putting on a facade. Hope a Mexican takes OPs job

62177482
>>62177365
>Tbqh from the outside sounds like you guys have the issue here. If there's something that's inherently confusing and held together by tribal knowledge you should fix this.
yeah na m8, once that shit is running the factory nobody gives a fuck, you just let it rip til the wheels fall off

62177837
Are you sure he actually did any of what you said at all? We had a problem here in Alberta when we were short on electricians years ago that anyone with a journeyman licence in another country could come here. We ended up with thousands of chinks who had never worked a day as an electrician

62177866
maybe you are the autistic one, he obviously doesn't like his role because he thinks he could do better, instead of finding a way to guide him, you want to come gossip on 4chan about it

62179764
>>62177866
> gossip on 4chan
You’re a literal idiot

>>62177347
This person is right >>62177365
Does your management actually give a shit or pay attention? You kind of make it sound like the way you do things that don’t fit the procedure are hidden from them.
Do they actually care or do they just not want to update SOPs?

62180674
>>62177347
I'm one of the manager guys that creates procedures for retards that don't follow them like OP cause "it's more efficient this way". I don't give a single fuck what you think nigger just do your job or get replaced

62181050
>>62180674
when's the last time your wife looked at you like you were someone she cared about?
